strategy_doc_section_loaded: Evaluations — Big Idea, Mood (deliberate), arc position; mood-arc table M1–M6; strategy doc content-strategy.md §Evaluations

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Lead with discipline, not failure mode
Strategic themes recur in Key Concepts
rules_evaluated.evidence: File has no ## Key Concepts section; it is a 3-4 min prose recognition beat with no concept list.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Key Concepts shape — theme breadcrumbs, not standalone section
rules_evaluated.evidence: File has no ## Key Concepts section; §6 evaluation requires Key Concepts bullets to exist.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: What You'll Learn shape — capability register, not syllabus
rules_evaluated.evidence: File has no ## What You'll Learn section; it is a recognition-beat lecture not a module file.

_eval_notes.rule_1_detail: Maintainer block names mood target as 'empowered recognition, not caution.' Strategy doc M6 mood is 'unleashed leverage — empowered builder.' Body closes with 'your judgment comes back in' — a leverage/empowerment frame, not a caution frame. Mood contract honored. Student leaves this beat in a state of clear-eyed recognition that the score's silence is information about the judge's scope, not a verdict on the work — then hands forward to new-human-role closer.
_eval_notes.rule_2_detail: Unease in this lecture, to the degree it exists, is epistemic: 'the easy signal runs out' frames uncertainty about what quality means beyond groundedness, not shame about having done something wrong. The line 'A grounded claim can still be vague, obvious, or useless, and this judge will pass it every time' frames the gap as a knowledge/frame gap, not a moral failure. Epistemic, not punitive.
_eval_notes.rule_3_detail: Body leads with 'The judge measures one thing' and closes with 'your judgment comes back in.' The positive discipline named throughout is: read past the metric, know what the judge can and cannot see, exercise human judgment at the boundary of automated signal. No failure words (fabrication, violation, hallucination) in the title or opening framing. 'Flatline' as a concept is introduced as information-about-frame, not as a negative verdict. Passes §3.
_eval_notes.rule_4_detail: The lecture's last line is 'That is the question the next lecture opens.' This is an explicit hand-off to new-human-role-in-the-loop.md, not a pre-emption. The question it hands forward (what is the residual human role?) is answered by the closer; this lecture delivers only the prerequisite insight (the judge's blind spot is real and bounded). Sequencing matches evaluations.md's inline ordering: eval-loop exercise → this lecture → new-human-role closer. Strategy doc confirms this sequencing. No front-running.
_eval_notes.rule_5_detail: N/A — no Key Concepts section. Agents 101 does not pin a fixed recurring-themes list (unlike AE101). The strategic-vocab gate for Agents 101 fires against the two-questions framing and mood-arc beats (evaluated under §1/§2 above). Body prose instances the Agents 101 strategic worldview — 'the work kept getting better in a direction the judge was never built to see' is a Compound/Sharpen pattern — but since no Key Concepts section exists, §5's bullet-level check does not fire.
_eval_notes.rule_6_detail: N/A — no Key Concepts section. §6 requires bullets to check for discipline-lead, downstream-front-running, and breadcrumb candidacy. None of those applies to a prose-only file.
_eval_notes.rule_7_detail: N/A — no What You'll Learn section. File is a 3-4 min recognition beat that follows an exercise; it belongs to the student who just ran a loop and sees the score flatten, not to anticipatory learning-objective framing.
